author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 21–25.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The lesson of the holy Gospel which preceded to-day’s had concluded thus: that “the Lord spake, teaching in the treasury,” what it pleased Him, and what you have heard; “and no one laid hands on Him, for His hour was not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, on the Lord’s day we made our subject of discourse what He Himself thought fit to give us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We indicated to your Charity why it was said, “His hour was not yet come,” lest any in their impiety should have the effrontery to suspect Christ as laid under some fatal necessity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the hour was not yet come when by His own appointment, in accordance with what was predicted regarding Him, He should not be forced to die unwillingly, but be ready to be slain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But of His own passion itself, which lay not in any necessity He was under, but in His own power, all that He said in His discourse to the Jews was, “I go away.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to Christ the Lord’s death was His proceeding to the place whence He had come, and from which He had never departed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I go away,” said He, “and ye shall seek me,” not from any longing for me, but in hatred.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after His removal from human sight, He was sought for both by those who hated Him and those who loved Him; by the former in a spirit of persecution, by the latter with the desire of having Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the Psalms the Lord Himself says by the prophet, “A place of refuge hath failed me, and there is none that seeketh after my life;” and again He says in another place in the Psalms, “Let them be confounded and ashamed who seek after my life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He blamed the former for not seeking, He condemned the latter because they did.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is wrong not to seek the life of Christ, that is, in the way the disciples sought it; and it is wrong to seek the life of Christ, that is, in the way the Jews sought it: for the former sought to possess it, these latter to destroy it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, because these men sought it thus in a wrong way, with a perverted heart, what next did He add?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall seek me, and”—not to let you suppose that ye will seek me for good—“ye shall die in your sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This comes of seeking Christ wrongly, to die in one’s sin; this of hating Him, through whom alone salvation could be found.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, while men whose hope is in God ought not to render evil even for evil, these men were rendering evil for good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord therefore announced to them beforehand, and in His foreknowledge uttered the sentence, that they should die in their sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He adds, “Whither I go, ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said the same to the disciples also in another place; and yet He said not to them, “Ye shall die in your sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what did He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same as to these men: “Whither I go, ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not take away hope, but foretold delay.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For at the time when the Lord spake this to the disciples, they were not able to come whither He was going, yet were they to come afterwards; but these men never, to whom in His foreknowledge He said, “Ye shall die in your sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But on hearing these words, as is usual with those whose thoughts are carnal, who judge after the flesh, and hear and apprehend everything in a carnal way, they said, “Will he kill himself because he said, Whither I go ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Foolish words, and overflowing with stupidity!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why could they not go whither He would have proceeded had He killed Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were not they themselves to die?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, means, “Will he kill himself because he said, Whither I go ye cannot come?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He spake of man’s death, what man is there that does not die?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, by “whither I go” He meant, not the going to death, but whither He was going Himself after death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such, then, was their answer, because they did not understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what said the Lord to those who savored of the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And He said unto them, Ye are from beneath.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this cause ye savor of the earth, because ye lick dust like serpents.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye eat earth!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does it mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye feed on earthly things, ye delight in earthly things, ye gape after earthly things, ye have no heart for what is above.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye are from beneath: I am from above.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye are of this world: I am not of this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how could He be of the world, by whom the world was made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All that are of the world come after the world, because the world preceded; and so man is of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ was first, and then the world; and since Christ was before the world, before Christ there was nothing: because “In the beginning was the Word; all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, therefore, was of that which is above.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But of what that is above?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perish the thought! there the birds wing their flight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the sky that we see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again I say, Perish the thought! it is there that the stars and sun and moon revolve.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the angels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither is this to be understood: by Him who made all things were the angels also made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what, then, above is Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the Father Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nothing is above that God who begat the Word equal with Himself, co-eternal with Himself, only-begotten, timeless, that by Him time’s own foundations should be laid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, then, Christ as from above, so as in thy thought to get beyond everything that is made,—the whole creation together, every material body, every created spirit, everything in any way subject to change: rise above all, as John rose, in order to reach this: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore said He, “I am from above.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye are of this world: I am not of this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has explained to us, brethren, what He wished to be understood by “ye are of this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said therefore in fact, “Ye are of this world,” because they were sinners, because they were unrighteous, because they were unbelieving, because they savored of the earthly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is your opinion as regards the holy apostles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What difference was there between the Jews and the apostles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As great as between darkness and light, as between faith and unbelief, as between piety and impiety, as between hope and despair, as between love and avarice: surely the difference was great.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, because there was such a difference, were the apostles not of the world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thy thoughts turn to the manner of their birth, and whence they came, inasmuch as all of them had come from Adam, they were of this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what said the Lord Himself to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have chosen you out of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those, then, who were of the world, became not of the world, and began to belong to Him by whom the world was made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these men continued to be of the world, to whom it was said, “Ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let none then, brethren, say, I am not of this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whoever thou art as a man, thou art of this world; but He who made the world came to thee, and delivered thee from this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the world delights thee, thou wishest always to be unclean (immundus); but if this world no longer delight thee, thou art already clean (mundus).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet, if through some infirmity the world still delight thee, let Him who cleanseth (mundat) dwell in thee, and thou too shalt be clean.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou art once clean, thou wilt not continue in the world; neither wilt thou hear what was heard by the Jews, “Ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are all born with sin; we have all in living added to that wherein we were born, and have since become more of the world than when we were born of our parents.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where should we be, had He not come, who was wholly free from sin, to expiate all sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, because in Him the Jews believed not, they deservedly heard [the sentence], “Ye shall die in your sins;” for in no way could ye, who were born with sin, be without sin; and yet, said He, if ye believe in me, although it is still true that ye were born with sin, yet in your sin ye shall not die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The whole misery, then, of the Jews was just this, not to have sin, but to die in their sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this it is that every Christian ought to seek to escape; because of this we have recourse to baptism; on this account do those whose lives are in danger from sickness or any other cause become anxious for help; for this also is the sucking child carried by his mother with pious hands to the church, that he may not go out into the world without baptism, and die in the sin wherein he was born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Most wretched surely the condition and miserable the lot of these men, who heard from those truth-speaking lips, “Ye shall die in your sins!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He explains whence this should befall them: “For if ye believe not that I am [He], ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I believe, brethren, that among the multitude who listened to the Lord, there were those also who should yet believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But against all, as it were, had that most severe sentence gone forth, “Ye shall die in your sin;” and thereby even from those who should yet believe had hope been withdrawn: the others were roused to fury, they to fear; yea, to more than fear, they were brought now to despair.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He revived their hope; for He added, “If ye believe not that I am, ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore if ye do believe that I am, ye shall not die in your sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hope was restored to the desponding, the sleeping were aroused, their hearts got a fresh awakening; and thereafter very many believed, as the Gospel itself attests in the sequel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For members of Christ were there, who had not yet become attached to the body of Christ; and among that people by whom He was crucified, by whom He was hanged on a tree, by whom when hanging He was mocked, by whom He was wounded with the spear, by whom gall and vinegar were given Him to drink, were the members of Christ, for whose sake He said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what will a convert not be forgiven, if the shedding of Christ’s blood is forgiven?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What murderer need despair, if he was restored to hope by whom even Christ was slain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After this many believed; they were presented with Christ’s blood as a gift, that they might drink it for their salvation, rather than be held guilty of shedding it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can despair?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if the thief was saved on the cross,—a murderer shortly before, a little afterwards accused, convicted, condemned, hanged, delivered,—wonder not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The place of his conviction was that of his condemnation; while that of his conversion was the place also of his deliverance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among this people, then, to whom the Lord was speaking, were those who should yet die in their sin: there were those also who should yet believe on Him who spake, and find deliverance from all their sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But look at this which is said by Christ the Lord: “If ye believe not that I am, ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, “If ye believe not that I am?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am” what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing added; and because He added nothing, He left much to be inferred.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was expected to say what He was, and yet He said it not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was He expected to say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps, “If ye believe not that I am” Christ; “if ye believe not that I am” the Son of God; “if ye believe not that I am” the Word of the Father; “if ye believe not that I am” the founder of the world; “if ye believe not that I am” the former and re-former, the creator and re-creator, the maker and re-maker of man;—“if ye believe not that I am” this, “ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is much implied in His only saying “I am;” for so also had God said to Moses, “I am who am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can adequately express what that am means?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God by His angel sent His servant Moses to deliver His people out of Egypt (you have read and know what you now hear; but I recall it to your minds); He sent him trembling, self-excusing, but obedient.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And while thus excusing himself, he said to God, whom he understood to be speaking in the person of the angel: If the people say to me, And who is the God that hath sent thee? what shall I say to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord answered him, “I am who am;” and added, “Thou shalt say to the children of Israel, He who is hath sent me to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There also He says not, I am God; or, I am the framer of the world; or, I am the creator of all things; or, I am the multiplier of the very people to be delivered: but only this, “I am who am;” and, “Thou shall say to the children of Israel, He who is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He added not, Who is your God, who is the God of your fathers; but said only this: “He who is hath sent me to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps it was too much even for Moses himself, as it is too much for us also, and much more so for us, to understand the meaning of such words, “I am who am;” and, “He who is hath sent me to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And supposing that Moses comprehended it, when would those to whom he was sent comprehend it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord therefore put aside what man could not comprehend, and added what he could; for He said also besides, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This thou canst comprehend; for “I am who am,” what mind can comprehend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then of us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall we venture to say anything on such words, “I am who am;” or rather on this, that you have heard the Lord saying, “If ye believe not that I am, ye shall die in your sins”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall I venture with these feeble and scarcely existing powers of mine to discuss the meaning of that which Christ the Lord hath said, “If ye believe not that I am”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I shall venture to ask the Lord Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to me as one asking rather than discussing, inquiring rather than assuming, learning rather than teaching, and fail not yourselves also to be asking with me or through me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Himself, who is everywhere, is also at hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Him hear the feeling that prompts to ask, and grant the fruit of understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in what words, even were it so that I comprehend something, can I convey to your hearts what I comprehend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What voice is adequate? what eloquence sufficient? what powers of intelligence? what faculty of utterance?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I shall speak, then, to our Lord Jesus Christ; I shall speak and may He be pleased to hear me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I believe He is present, I am fully assured of it; for He Himself has said, “Lo, I am with you even to the end of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Lord our God, what is that which Thou saidst, “If ye believe not that I am”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is there that belongs not to the things Thou hast made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not heaven so belong?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not everything in earth and heaven?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not man himself to whom Thou speakest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not the angel whom Thou sendest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If all these are things made by Thee, what is that existence Thou hast retained as something exclusively Thine own, which Thou hast given to none besides, that Thou mightest be such Thyself alone?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how do I hear “I am who am,” as if there were none besides? and how do I hear “If ye believe not that I am”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had they no existence who heard Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, though they were sinners, they were men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then can I do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What that existence is, let Him tell my heart, let Him tell, let Him declare it within; let the inner man hear, the mind apprehend this true existence; for such existence is always unvarying in character.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For a thing, anything whatever (I have begun as it were to dispute, and have left off inquiring.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps I wish to speak what I have heard.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He grant enlargement to my hearing, and to yours, while I speak);—for anything, whatever in short be its excellence, if it is changeable, does not truly exist; for there is no true existence wherever non-existence has also a place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whatever can be changed, so far as changed, it is not that which was: if it is no longer what it was, a kind of death has therein taken place; something that was there has been eliminated, and exists no more.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Blackness has died out in the silvery locks of the patriarch, comeliness in the body of the careworn and crooked old man, strength in the body of the languishing, the [previous] standing posture in the body of one walking, walking in the body of one standing, walking and standing in the body of one reclining, speech in the tongue of the silent;—whatever changes, and is what it was not, I see there a kind of life in that which is, and death in that which was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In fine, when we say of one deceased, Where is that person? we are answered, He was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Truth, it is thou [alone] that truly art!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in all actions and movements of ours, yea, in every activity of the creature, I find two times, the past and the future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I seek for the present, nothing stands still: what I have said is no longer present; what I am going to say is not yet come: what I have done is no longer present; what I am going to do is not yet come: the life I have lived is no longer present; the life I have still to live is not yet come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Past and future I find in every creature-movement: in truth, which is abiding, past and future I find not, but the present alone, and that unchangeably, which has no place in the creature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sift the mutations of things, thou wilt find was and will be: think on God, thou wilt find the is, where was and will be cannot exist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To be so then thyself, rise beyond the boundaries of time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who can transcend the powers of his being?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He raise us thither who said to the Father, “I will that they also be with me where I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, in making this promise, that we should not die in our sins, the Lord Jesus Christ, I think, said nothing else by these words, “If ye believe not that I am;” yea, by these words I think He meant nothing else than this, “If ye believe not that I am” God, “ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, God be thanked that He said, “If ye believe not,” and did not say, If ye comprehend not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who can comprehend this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is it so, since I have ventured to speak and you have seemed to understand, that you have indeed comprehended somewhat of a subject so unspeakable?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then thou comprehendest not, faith sets thee free.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore also the Lord said not, If ye comprehend not that I am; but said what they were capable of attaining, “If ye believe not that I am, ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And savoring as these men always did of the earth, and ever hearing and answering according to the flesh, what did they say to Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Who art thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when thou saidst, “If ye believe not that I am,” thou didst not tell us what thou wert.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who art thou, that we may believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answered “The Beginning.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here is the existence that [always] is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The beginning cannot be changed: the beginning is self-abiding and all-originating; that is, the beginning, to which it has been said, “But thou Thyself art the same, and Thy years shall not fail.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The beginning,” He said, “for so I also speak to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believe me [to be] the beginning, that ye may not die in your sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For just as if by saying, “Who art thou?” they had said nothing else than this, What shall we believe thee to be?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He replied, “The beginning;” that is, Believe me [to be] the “beginning.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the Greek expression we discern what we cannot in the Latin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in Greek the word “beginning” (principium, ἀρχή), is of the feminine gender, just as with us “law” (lex) is of the feminine gender, while it is of the masculine (νόμος) with them; or as “wisdom” (sapientia, σοφία) is of the feminine gender with both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the custom of speech, therefore, in different languages to vary the gender of words, because in things themselves there is no place for the distinction of sex.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For wisdom is not really female, since Christ is the Wisdom of God, and Christ is termed of the masculine gender, wisdom of the feminine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When then the Jews said, “Who art thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, who knew that there were some there who should yet believe, and therefore had said, Who art thou? that so they might come to know what they ought to believe regarding Him, replied, “The beginning:” not as if He said, I am the beginning; but as if He said, Believe me [to be] the beginning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which, as I said, is quite evident in the Greek language, where beginning (ἀρχή) is of the feminine gender.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as if He had wished to say that He was the Truth, and to their question, “Who art thou?” had answered, Veritatem[the Truth]; when to the words, “Who art thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He evidently ought to have replied, Veritas [the Truth]; that is, I am the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But His answer had a deeper meaning, when He saw that they had put the question, “Who art thou?” in such a way as to mean, Having heard from thee, “If ye believe not that I am,” what shall we believe thee to be?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this He replied, “The beginning:” as if He said, Believe me to be the beginning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He added “for [as such] I also speak to you;” that is, having humbled myself on your account, I have condescended to such words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the beginning as it is in itself had remained so with the Father, as not to receive the form of a servant and speak as man with men; how could they have believed in Him, since their weak hearts could not have heard the Word intelligently without some voice that would appeal to their senses?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, said He, believe me to be the beginning; for, that you may believe, I not only am, but also speak to you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But on this subject I have still much to say to you; may it therefore please your Charity that we reserve what remains, and by His gracious aid deliver it tomorrow.
